data.title: ACC trims substantial stake in NZX Limited
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NZX ( (NZSTF) ) has shared an update.

Accident Compensation Corporation has reduced its substantial shareholding in NZX Limited, selling 3,603,095 ordinary shares on-market between December 2025 and April 2026. Following these transactions, ACC’s holding fell from 22,215,170 to 18,612,075 shares, lowering its stake from 6.762% to 5.653% of NZX’s quoted voting products.

The reduction in ACC’s stake represents a sizeable divestment by a major institutional investor in New Zealand’s stock exchange operator. While ACC remains a substantial product holder, the move may signal portfolio rebalancing or changing investment priorities, and is likely to be closely watched by market participants given NZX’s role at the core of the country’s capital markets.

More about NZX

NZX Limited operates New Zealand’s primary securities exchange, providing listing, trading and market services for equities and other financial products. The company plays a central role in the country’s capital markets infrastructure, facilitating investment, price discovery and liquidity for issuers and investors.

See more insights into NZSTF stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Neuren Pharmaceuticals Limited ( (AU:NEU) ) has shared an update.

Neuren Pharmaceuticals has issued an online meeting guide for shareholders and appointed proxies ahead of an upcoming virtual shareholder meeting. The guide details technical requirements, including supported browsers and necessary identification such as shareholder or proxy numbers, and explains how investors can register, access the live webcast, view presentation slides, obtain electronic voting cards, and submit full or partial votes on meeting resolutions, underscoring the company’s move to streamline and formalize remote participation in its governance processes.

An update from Volt Power Group Ltd ( (AU:VPR) ) is now available.

Volt Group Limited has called its 2026 annual general meeting for 28 May 2026 in Kewdale, Western Australia, setting 26 May 2026 as the record date for shareholders eligible to vote. The meeting will consider the company’s financial, directors’ and auditors’ reports for the year ended 31 December 2025, with these materials provided in the latest annual report.

Shareholders will also vote on an advisory resolution to adopt the 2025 remuneration report, which, while non-binding, will guide the board’s future pay policies. The notice outlines voting restrictions for key management personnel and their related parties, reinforcing governance standards around executive remuneration decisions.

data.title: Neuren Files Updated ASX Governance Appendix, Confirms Compliance With Key Standards
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Neuren Pharmaceuticals Limited ( (AU:NEU) ) just unveiled an update.

Neuren Pharmaceuticals has lodged its Appendix 4G and confirmed that its corporate governance statement for the year ended 31 December 2025 is included in its latest annual report and current as of 31 March 2026. The filing, authorised by CFO and Company Secretary Lauren Frazer, affirms that the company follows the ASX Corporate Governance Council’s key recommendations, including a formal board charter, director vetting and written agreements for directors and executives.

The disclosures indicate that Neuren’s company secretary reports directly to the board through the chair, reinforcing the board’s oversight of governance processes. By mapping where each governance disclosure can be found, the Appendix 4G serves as both a transparency tool for investors and a compliance check, underscoring Neuren’s adherence to ASX listing rules and established governance standards.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:NEU) stock is a Buy with a A$19.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Neuren Pharmaceuticals Limited stock, see the AU:NEU Stock Forecast page.

Neuren Pharmaceuticals Limited is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing therapies for neurological disorders. Listed on the ASX, it operates under Australian corporate governance requirements and reports annually on its governance practices to investors and regulators.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Suncorp Group ( (AU:SUN) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Suncorp Group has launched a Wholesale Note Issuance Programme to issue Additional Tier 1 Capital Notes and Tier 2 Subordinated Notes to wholesale investors. The framework is designed to give the group flexibility to raise regulatory capital and funding on an ongoing basis, supporting its balance sheet strength and capital management strategy amid evolving prudential requirements.

The notes issued under the programme will not be offered to U.S. persons and are structured to comply with securities regulations in key jurisdictions, including Australia and Singapore. By targeting non-retail, professional markets, Suncorp aims to broaden its access to institutional funding while ensuring adherence to complex cross-border capital markets rules and investor eligibility criteria.

An update from Centuria Capital Group ( (AU:CNI) ) is now available.

Centuria Capital Group has disclosed a change in director John Richard Slater’s indirect holding in the company’s fully paid stapled securities. The change arises from transactions executed through entities associated with his family investment structures.

According to the notice, a related entity, JRSWJH Pty Ltd as trustee for The Slater Family Super Fund, disposed of 400,000 stapled securities on-market on 20 April 2026 for total consideration of $626,878. Following the sale, Slater’s indirect interests via his family entities remain substantial, suggesting an ongoing alignment with Centuria’s long-term performance despite the partial selldown.

data.title: Energy Resources of Australia Discloses Director’s Updated Rio Tinto Equity Holdings
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Energy Resources of Australia Class A ( (AU:ERA) ) has issued an update.

Energy Resources of Australia has disclosed a change in director Justin Carey’s indirect interests in Rio Tinto Limited securities, stemming from his participation in Rio Tinto’s global employee share plan. Carey received additional Rio Tinto shares through the vesting of matching share rights and made further share purchases under the plan, slightly increasing his indirect holdings and adjusting the number of conditional matching rights he holds.

The transaction involved the vesting and issue of matching share rights with no cash consideration for the vested rights, while shares purchased under the plan were acquired at a set market price. The change signals routine evolution in Carey’s equity-based remuneration within the Rio Tinto group framework, without indicating any broader strategic shift or contractual change for Energy Resources of Australia itself.

More about Energy Resources of Australia Class A

Energy Resources of Australia Ltd is an Australian resources company associated with Rio Tinto, with its director Justin Carey participating in Rio Tinto’s global employee share and equity incentive programs. Through these schemes, Carey holds Rio Tinto Limited shares and associated rights, reflecting a remuneration structure tied to the broader Rio Tinto group’s performance.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$1.62B

data.title: Napier Port Sets Date for FY2026 Half-Year Results and Investor Briefing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Napier Port Holdings Ltd ( (NZ:NPH) ) just unveiled an update.

Napier Port will release its financial results for the half year ended 31 March 2026 on the morning of 20 May 2026, providing the market with an update on trading and financial performance at the midpoint of its fiscal year. As a major export gateway for Hawke’s Bay and the lower North Island, these results will offer insight into regional trade flows and the port’s operational momentum.

Following the announcement, Chair Blair O’Keeffe, Chief Executive Todd Dawson and Chief Financial Officer Kristen Lie will host a conference call at 11:00am NZT to discuss the results in more detail. Presentation materials will be made available via the NZX and the company’s investor centre, enabling investors and analysts to review the performance data and management commentary in a structured format.

The scheduled briefing underlines Napier Port’s emphasis on transparency and engagement with the investment community. Stakeholders will gain greater visibility into how the port is tracking against its strategic objectives and how broader economic conditions and cargo trends are influencing the business.

More about Napier Port Holdings Ltd

Napier Port Holdings Ltd operates Napier Port, New Zealand’s fourth-largest port by container volume, serving as the key gateway for exports from Hawke’s Bay and the lower North Island. The company runs critical regional infrastructure that underpins the local economy and focuses on collaborating with regional stakeholders to support long-term growth.

Average Trading Volume: 59,998

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy
